acceptance_criteria: On a tick against a project store carrying a prose intent that resolves to no
A parity check: the prose-entry interactive tick and the autonomous path
The `go --work-unit` and `go --ticket` contracts and their exit-code semantics
No new `SessionScope` variant is introduced: the prose-entry session evaluates
description:
## Summary

`runa go` reaches an unscoped planning protocol only through the **reference**
entry — `go --ticket <REF>` opens a cold-start session at the methodology's
acquisition surface (`decompose`, the unscoped `work-unit` producer) and
materializes a `work-unit` from the ticket. The **prose** entry has no operator
surface: an intent that carries prose and *no resolvable referent* must enter
`survey`, and `survey` runs unscoped by definition — it examines the exigence
before any `work-unit` exists — but no `go` invocation drives it. An interactive
operator holding a prose intent (a question, a feature idea, an unfamiliar
territory to assess) cannot start the methodology at `survey` through the
validated single-tick surface.

This is the **prose → survey** route of the settled entry spectrum, made
operator-drivable.

## Where this sits (grounded 2026-07-01)

The entry design settled since this issue was filed. The current architecture
routes this issue precisely:

- **ADR-0001 Decision 2** defines three entry routes off a single
  resolve-and-materialize discipline, and assigns this issue to **Route 3**:
  *"A request carrying prose and no resolvable referent enters `survey` … (the
  existing `request` → `survey` path, drivable unscoped per #174)."*
- **Route 1** (reference → acquire/execute) is **landed** as #188 — this is the
  `go --ticket` cold-start path now in `go.rs::run_ticket`
  (`entry::discover_acquisition_surface` → `decompose`). It is **not** what this
  issue tracks.
- The `request → intent` rename (tesserine/commons#94) and the operator-seeding
  model (ADR-0002 / #214: `wish` authors, `go` advances) landed at the contract
  layer. **The CLI surface at HEAD is still `go (--work-unit | --ticket)`** —
  no `wish` verb, no prose/survey entry. This route is genuinely unbuilt.

## What is already true (grounded in runa's code at HEAD 14f8526)

- `go` dispatches `(work_unit, ticket)`: `--work-unit` → `run_bound` (scoped
  tick), `--ticket` → `run_ticket` (cold-start acquisition, unscoped, no
  work-unit id). Both route through the same readiness evaluation
  (`evaluate_execution_state` → `discover_ready_candidates`); mode changes only
  who issues the verb at what cadence, never its meaning (ADR-0001 D1;
  session-surface-contract).
- `run_ticket` reaches an **unscoped** protocol (`decompose`) through the
  validated surface — proving the unscoped tick is already a solved shape at the
  MCP/session layer. What it does *not* do is enter at `survey`: it enters at the
  `work-unit` producer and requires a referent to materialize.
- `survey` (groundwork `protocols/survey/PROTOCOL.md`) consumes an `intent`
  artifact and produces `requirements`; it is unscoped and is the pipeline entry
  point for prose intent. `decompose` consumes `requirements` and produces
  `work-unit`. Neither is reachable from `go` on a prose-only intent today.

## Substrate — where the seam actually is (grounded at HEAD 14f8526)

Three findings from plan review, folded in so the implementation reuses what
exists rather than reintroducing a parallel mechanism. These are the load-bearing
constraints on HOW:

- **`EvaluationScope::Unscoped` already exists and is already wired — reuse it,
  do not add a scope variant.** `libagent/src/session.rs` `SessionScope` has two
  variants, `Bound(String)` and `Promised { ticket }`; `evaluation_scope()` maps
  `Promised → EvaluationScope::Unscoped`, `context_work_unit()` maps
  `Promised → None`, and `Promised` runs the identical
  `reconcile_after_scan` / `select_next` / postcondition cascade. Every property a
  prose-entry session needs (unscoped evaluation, `work_unit = None`, normal
  readiness/postcondition/execution-record logic) is already the behavior of the
  unscoped evaluation path. Adding a third `SessionScope` variant that duplicates
  those semantics is a **third mode** (forbidden below) and a single-home
  violation the module's own doc guards ("no driver reimplements readiness").
- **The gap is one missing *open path*, not a new scope.** `Promised` is unscoped
  but **ticket-pinned and acquisition-pinned**: `open_entry` pins the step to
  `discover_acquisition_surface` (`decompose`, the work-unit producer) and
  requires a `TicketRef`. Prose entry is unscoped and **survey-pinned with no
  referent** — it must let readiness select the first ready unscoped step
  (`survey`, the `intent → requirements` producer) with no ticket and no
  work-unit. No current constructor opens an unscoped session from a prose intent
  with no referent: `open`/`open_with_validator` reject `work_unit = None` with
  `SessionError::MissingWorkUnit`, and `open_entry` demands a ticket. That missing
  open path — carrying no `TicketRef`, admitting `survey` via general unscoped
  readiness — is the unit of work.
- **MCP `--session` already parses no-selector; the blocker is downstream.**
  `runa-mcp --session` already accepts no selector (`--session` is a bare bool,
  `--work-unit` independent/optional; `main.rs` routes the no-ticket/no-work-unit
  case to `new_session(.., cli.work_unit, ..)`). The blocker is not tool
  exposure: `new_session` → `open_with_validator_and_identity` → `open` →
  `work_unit.ok_or(MissingWorkUnit)`. The fix is the prose-entry open path above,
  consumed by `new_session` when `work_unit` is `None` and the store holds a prose
  intent.

The two-layer shape is load-bearing: the CLI `go` layer drives the agent via
`evaluate_execution_state` + `execute_entry` + `session_advance_receipt_matches`
(as `run_ticket` does — **not** via a `SessionState` constructor), while the
`libagent::SessionState` machinery is the MCP-side session. The prose-entry path
threads three thin layers: the `SessionState` open path (admitting `survey`) →
MCP `new_session` no-selector consumption → CLI `go` `(None, None)` arm.

## What must be true

- An interactive operator can advance `survey` for a project whose store holds a
  prose intent with **no resolvable referent** and **no work-unit yet**, through
  the same single-tick `go` discipline used for scoped work and for `--ticket`
  acquisition — producing a validated `requirements` artifact through the
  methodology's output tool.
- The prose-entry tick reaches the same validated cascade (assess → select →
  context → output recording → postcondition-gated commit) as the scoped and
  acquisition ticks; the route is a **derivation of assessed state at the entry
  boundary** (ADR-0001 D1), not a new verb or a third mode.
- The scoped `go --work-unit` path and the `--ticket` acquisition path are
  unchanged.
- The prose-entry path reuses the existing `EvaluationScope::Unscoped` evaluation
  path; it introduces **no new `SessionScope` variant** and no new readiness,
  context-delivery, or transition-authority logic. It is a new open/entry path
  over the existing unscoped evaluation, not a parallel mode beside it.

## Acceptance criteria

- On a tick against a project store carrying a prose intent that resolves to no
  referent, `go` advances a READY unscoped `survey` protocol and produces a
  validated `requirements` artifact through the methodology's output tool — with
  no work-unit id supplied.
- A parity check: the prose-entry interactive tick and the autonomous path
  produce identical recorded artifacts / execution metadata for the same
  `survey` step (the AC4 parity pattern from #168, extended to the prose-entry
  scope).
- The `go --work-unit` and `go --ticket` contracts and their exit-code semantics
  are unchanged.
- No new `SessionScope` variant is introduced: the prose-entry session evaluates
  through the existing `EvaluationScope::Unscoped` path. (Verifiable by inspection
  of `libagent/src/session.rs`: the scope enum's variant set is unchanged; the
  prose-entry open path constructs an unscoped session that reuses
  `evaluation_scope()`'s existing unscoped mapping.)

## Scope boundary — what this is NOT

- **Not the `--ticket` acquisition path.** That is Route 1 / #188, landed.
- **Not the seed-`target` read engine.** #217 (retire `--ticket`, read the
  intent's `target` from assessed store state) needs the entry-derivation engine
  that reads a *reference* carried in the seed — ADR-0001 D1's last unbuilt
  piece on the **reference** side. That engine is a distinct, currently-unfiled
  unit; #217's blocker should point at it, not at this issue. This issue is the
  **prose** side of the same boundary (Route 3), independent of the seed-target
  read.

## Sequencing

On the dual-mode entry surface (parent #167), in the unscoped/planning-entry
region — phase-2-adjacent, not the phase-1 scoped critical path. It does not
gate the commons#48 ecosystem release (that ships on the scoped and acquisition
surfaces). Sequence against M1 is the operator's call.

Parent: #167 (dual-mode epic). Architecture: ADR-0001 (entry spectrum),
ADR-0002 (operator seeding).
title: task(entry): runa go drives the prose→survey route unscoped (Route 3)
